entries.title: Forged instructions sysvar bypass
entries.description: Wormhole bridge used forged sysvar account to bypass signature verification of transfer instructions. $320M loss.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker constructs a transaction with a forged sysvar account that passes the program's verification check, allowing unauthorized token transfers.

entries.title: Missing account validation in collateral backing
entries.description: Cashio failed to validate collateral accounts, allowing minting of unbacked CASH tokens. $52M loss.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker passes fake collateral accounts that are not validated for ownership or type, minting CASH without real backing.

entries.title: Forged price tick account via CPI
entries.description: Crema allowed attacker to report fake liquidity price via forged tick account in CPI call. $8.8M loss.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker constructs a fake tick account and passes it to the CPI call. Since program_id of the tick account is not verified, the fake account reports manipulated prices.

entries.title: Price manipulation via oracle feed
entries.description: Mango Markets exploited via price manipulation of oracle feeds, allowing attacker to drain liquidity. $114M loss.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker manipulates the oracle price feed by taking opposing positions, triggering liquidations at favorable prices.

entries.title: Economic exploit via bonding curve invariant
entries.description: Nirvana Finance bonding curve exploited for risk-free profit due to invariant mismatch. $3.5M loss.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker exploits a mismatch between the bonding curve invariant and the reward calculation to extract risk-free profit.

entries.title: Missing signer check on admin withdrawal
entries.description: Admin withdrawal function does not verify is_signer, allowing anyone to call it.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker calls the admin withdrawal function without being a signer, draining program funds.

entries.title: Arbitrary CPI without program_id verification
entries.description: Program invokes another program via CPI without verifying the target program_id.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker deploys a malicious program with the same interface. When the vulnerable program invokes CPI, the malicious program executes instead.

entries.title: PDA seed collision via manipulable input
entries.description: Program derives PDA using user-controlled input as seed without validation, allowing PDA substitution.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker crafts input that produces a PDA colliding with a legitimate account, gaining access to its funds.

entries.title: Unchecked integer overflow in token amount
entries.description: Token transfer amount calculation uses unchecked arithmetic, potential overflow on large values.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker passes large values that cause overflow, resulting in small actual transfer while crediting large amount.

entries.title: Reentrancy via chained CPI state update
entries.description: Program updates state after CPI call, allowing callee to observe stale state and exploit it.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker's program is invoked via CPI, sees stale state, and exploits the inconsistency before the caller updates.

entries.title: Missing owner check on token account withdrawal
entries.description: Program withdraws from token accounts without verifying the owner field matches the expected SPL Token program.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker creates a fake token account with the same structure but different owner, passes it to the withdrawal function.

entries.title: Uninitialized account used as authority
entries.description: Program checks authority field on an account that may not be initialized, defaulting to zero address which matches system program.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker passes an uninitialized account whose authority field defaults to zero, bypassing the authority check.

entries.title: Missing rent exemption check on created accounts
entries.description: Program creates accounts without verifying they are rent-exempt, allowing garbage collection of critical state.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker creates accounts with minimal lamports that get garbage collected, losing program state.

entries.title: Signer check bypass via unchecked remaining_accounts
entries.description: Program iterates remaining_accounts without verifying signer status for privileged operations.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker includes non-signer accounts in remaining_accounts that are used for privileged operations.

entries.title: Account discriminator mismatch on deserialization
entries.description: Program deserializes accounts without checking the Anchor discriminator, allowing type confusion.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker passes an account of the wrong type that has compatible data layout, causing type confusion.

entries.title: Close account without returning lamports
entries.description: Program closes accounts but lamports are not returned to the owner, effectively burning user funds.
entries.exploit_scenario: Program closes user accounts and lamports go to the program instead of the rightful owner.

entries.title: Missing close authority check
entries.description: Account close function does not verify the close authority matches the original depositor.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker calls the close function on another user's account, draining their lamports.

entries.title: PDA not re-derived in instruction handler
entries.description: Program accepts a PDA as input without re-deriving it from canonical seeds, allowing arbitrary PDA substitution.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker passes a different PDA than the one derived from canonical seeds, redirecting funds.

entries.title: Missing bump seed in PDA verification
entries.description: Program verifies PDA seeds but not the bump, allowing collision attacks with different bump values.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker finds a PDA with different bump that collides with the canonical PDA address.

entries.title: Unchecked return value from SPL token transfer
entries.description: Program ignores the return value of SPL token transfer CPI, failing silently on errors.
entries.exploit_scenario: Token transfer fails but program continues as if successful, crediting the user without actual transfer.

entries.title: Flash loan price manipulation in AMM
entries.description: AMM uses spot price from a single pool without flash loan protection, allowing price manipulation.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker takes a flash loan, manipulates pool price, exploits dependent protocol, repays loan.

entries.title: Replay attack on signed instructions
entries.description: Program processes signed instructions without nonce tracking, allowing replay of valid signatures.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker captures a valid signed instruction and replays it multiple times.

entries.title: Missing deadline parameter on swap
entries.description: Swap function has no deadline check, allowing pending transactions to be sandwiched.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker front-runs a pending swap, manipulates price, then back-runs for profit.

entries.title: Slippage protection bypass via zero minimum
entries.description: Swap allows minimum_out=0, disabling slippage protection entirely.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker sandwiches a swap with zero minimum output, extracting maximum value.

entries.title: Oracle staleness check missing
entries.description: Program uses oracle price without checking the update timestamp, allowing stale price exploitation.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker exploits a stale oracle price that doesn't reflect current market conditions.

entries.title: Integer overflow in reward calculation
entries.description: Staking reward calculation multiplies amount by rate without overflow check, allowing infinite rewards.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker stakes maximum amount, causing overflow in reward calculation, receiving inflated rewards.

entries.title: Division by zero in fee calculation
entries.description: Fee calculation divides by total_supply without checking for zero, causing panic and DoS.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker drains the pool to zero supply, then triggers fee calculation causing panic.

entries.title: Unchecked arithmetic in balance update
entries.description: Balance update uses wrapping subtraction without underflow check, allowing wrap-around to max value.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker triggers a subtraction that underflows, wrapping balance to u64::MAX.

entries.title: Precision loss in ratio calculation
entries.description: Token ratio calculation truncates before multiplication, causing systematic fund loss.
entries.exploit_scenario: Repeated small trades exploit truncation, extracting value over time.

entries.title: Missing freeze authority check on token mint
entries.description: Program accepts a token mint without verifying the freeze authority is the expected program.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker creates a token mint with themselves as freeze authority, freezing user accounts.

entries.title: CPI guard missing on user-facing instruction
entries.description: Program instruction allows arbitrary CPI calls without restricting the target program.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker uses the program as a CPI proxy to call arbitrary programs with the program's authority.

entries.title: Missing account size validation on resize
entries.description: Program resizes accounts without validating the new size, allowing excessive allocation.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker resizes an account to maximum size, consuming excessive rent and causing DoS.

entries.title: Cross-program account confusion via same layout
entries.description: Two programs use the same account data layout, and neither checks the owner field, allowing cross-program account reuse.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker passes an account from program A to program B, which deserializes it as its own type.

entries.title: Admin function without timelock
entries.description: Admin can change critical parameters instantly without a timelock, allowing rug pulls.
entries.exploit_scenario: Compromised admin key instantly changes fee to 100%, draining user funds.

entries.title: Missing multisig for admin operations
entries.description: Admin operations require only a single signer, creating a single point of failure.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker compromises the single admin key and drains all funds.

entries.title: Unchecked delegation in token transfer
entries.description: Program transfers tokens from delegated accounts without verifying delegation amount.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker exploits a stale delegation that should have been revoked, transferring more than authorized.

entries.title: Associated token account not verified
entries.description: Program accepts token accounts without verifying they are the correct ATA for the user and mint.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker passes a different ATA, causing tokens to be sent to the wrong recipient.

entries.title: Missing check on mint authority
entries.description: Program accepts a mint without verifying the mint authority is the expected program or multisig.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker creates a fake mint with themselves as authority, minting tokens that the program treats as valid.

entries.title: Swap route manipulation via unverified oracle
entries.description: Aggregator uses an on-chain price oracle that can be manipulated by flash loans for route selection.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker manipulates oracle price to force aggregator to pick a suboptimal route, extracting arbitrage.

entries.title: Liquidity pool drain via imbalanced withdrawal
entries.description: Withdrawal function allows removing only one side of the LP pair, breaking the invariant.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker withdraws only the valuable token from the LP, leaving worthless tokens as backing.

entries.title: Missing invariant check after state mutation
entries.description: Program mutates pool state without verifying invariants hold after the operation.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker crafts a sequence of operations that leaves the pool in an inconsistent state for exploitation.

entries.title: Front-running on liquidation
entries.description: Liquidation function is front-runnable, allowing MEV extraction from liquidators.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker front-runs a liquidation transaction, claiming the liquidation bonus themselves.

entries.title: Missing collateral ratio check after price update
entries.description: Lending protocol does not recheck collateral ratio after oracle price update within the same transaction.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker updates oracle price and borrows in the same transaction, bypassing the collateral ratio check.

entries.title: Unchecked metadata URL in NFT mint
entries.description: NFT mint accepts arbitrary metadata URLs without validation, allowing malicious external references.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker sets metadata URL to a malicious server that serves different content to different viewers.

entries.title: Royalty bypass via direct transfer
entries.description: NFT marketplace enforces royalties only on marketplace trades, not on direct transfers.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker transfers NFT directly to buyer off-marketplace, bypassing royalty payments.

entries.title: Missing token-2022 extension check
entries.description: Program uses SPL Token but does not account for Token-2022 extensions like transfer fees.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker uses Token-2022 with transfer fees, causing balance mismatches in the program's accounting.

entries.title: Authority escalation via unchecked update
entries.description: Update function allows setting authority to any value without verifying the new authority is valid.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker calls update with their own pubkey as the new authority, taking over the program.

entries.title: Missing event emission on state change
entries.description: Critical state changes do not emit events, making off-chain monitoring impossible.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker exploits the lack of monitoring by making undetected state changes.

entries.title: Unsafe type cast in amount calculation
entries.description: Program casts u128 to u64 without checking for truncation, losing high bits on large values.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker crafts inputs that produce a u128 value exceeding u64::MAX, truncating to a small value.

entries.title: Missing check on program upgrade authority
entries.description: Program interacts with another program without verifying its upgrade authority is known/expected.
entries.exploit_scenario: Target program is upgraded to a malicious version, and the calling program still trusts it.

entries.title: Stale PDA bump in seed derivation
entries.description: Program stores PDA bump and uses it in seeds instead of calling find_program_address, allowing bump manipulation.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker manipulates the stored bump value, causing the PDA to resolve to a different address.

entries.title: Missing validation on user-supplied program ID
entries.description: Program accepts a program ID as input and invokes CPI to it without whitelisting.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker supplies their own malicious program ID, which is invoked via CPI with the caller's authority.

entries.title: Economic griefing via dust amounts
entries.description: Protocol allows dust deposits that trigger full reward calculations, causing economic griefing.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker makes many dust deposits, each triggering full reward accrual, draining the reward pool.

entries.title: Missing re-initialization guard
entries.description: Init function can be called multiple times, resetting state and allowing authority takeover.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker calls init on an already-initialized account, resetting the authority to themselves.

entries.title: Cross-instance replay of signed message
entries.description: Signed message does not include the program ID or instance ID, allowing replay across different deployments.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker takes a valid signature from one deployment and replays it on another.

entries.title: Missing check on associated token account owner
entries.description: Program verifies the ATA address but not that the account owner matches the expected user.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker creates an ATA for a different user and passes it, redirecting tokens.

entries.title: Unchecked conversion fee in swap path
entries.description: Multi-hop swap does not account for conversion fees between hops, causing output shortfall.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker exploits the fee accounting gap to extract value from multi-hop swaps.

entries.title: Missing domain separation in signature verification
entries.description: Signature verification does not include a domain separator, allowing cross-protocol signature reuse.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker reuses a signature from a different protocol that uses the same signing scheme.

entries.title: Reward calculation uses stale total supply
entries.description: Reward per token is calculated using a cached total supply that may be stale, allowing inflated rewards.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker deposits after total supply changes but before the cache updates, receiving inflated rewards.

entries.title: Missing check on instruction discriminant in fallback
entries.description: Program's fallback handler processes instructions without checking the discriminant, allowing unintended execution paths.
entries.exploit_scenario: Attacker sends an instruction with an unknown discriminant that falls through to the fallback handler.
